workflow.name: Scan business cards from LINE with Gemini to Sheets, Slack, and Gmail
workflow.description:
## Who is this for

This workflow is designed for sales professionals, account managers, and small business owners in Japan who frequently exchange business cards. Instead of manually entering contact details, you can instantly capture and store them by sending a photo to LINE.

## What this workflow does

When a user sends a business card photo via LINE, the workflow uses Google Gemini Vision to extract key contact details — name, company, title, email, phone, and address — and saves them as a new row in Google Sheets. The team is notified in Slack, and a thank-you email is automatically sent to the new contact via Gmail. Finally, a confirmation message is sent back to the user on LINE.

## How to set up

1. Connect your LINE Messaging API credentials in n8n
2. Set your LINE channel webhook URL in the LINE Developer Console
3. Add your Google Gemini API key (free tier available)
4. Share your target Google Sheet with the service account email
5. Connect your Slack workspace and set the target channel
6. Connect your Gmail account

## Requirements

- LINE Messaging API account (free)
- Google Gemini API key (free tier)
- Google Sheets (any Google account)
- Slack workspace
- Gmail account

## How to customize

Change the Gemini prompt in the "Extract card data" node to capture additional fields. Modify the Slack message format or Gmail template to match your team's tone.

workflow.name: Scan business cards with LINE and Gemini, then send thank-you emails via Gmail

assignments.value: 名刺を登録しました！Google Sheetsに保存されています。

parameters.message:
=Dear {{ $('Parse Gemini JSON response').item.json.full_name }},

Thank you for connecting with us. It was a pleasure meeting you.

We have saved your contact information and will be in touch soon.

Best regards
parameters.subject: ={{ $('Set config values').item.json.config.thankYouEmailSubject }}

nodes.name: Extract business card data with Gemini

parameters.text:
=You are a business card OCR assistant.
Extract information from the business card image and return ONLY a valid JSON object with no additional text, markdown, or explanation.

Required JSON format:
{
  "full_name": "",
  "company": "",
  "department": "",
  "title": "",
  "email": "",
  "phone": "",
  "address": "",
  "website": "",
  "notes": ""
}

Rules:
- If a field is not found, use an empty string ""
- For phone, include country code if visible (e.g. +81-3-xxxx-xxxx)
- For Japanese names, romanize if only kanji/kana is present
- Return only the JSON object, nothing else

{{ $json.data }}

parameters.content:
Got a stack of meishi from your last networking event? Just snap a photo and send it to LINE — this workflow handles the rest.

### How it works

The LINE webhook picks up your image, checks it's actually a photo (not a text message), then passes it straight to Gemini Vision for OCR. Gemini pulls out the key fields — name, company, title, email, phone, address — and the result gets appended as a new row in your Google Sheet. Meanwhile, Slack gets a ping with the contact summary, Gmail fires off a thank-you email if an address was found, and LINE confirms back to you that it's all saved.

### Setup steps

1. Add your LINE Messaging API credentials in n8n and paste the webhook URL into the LINE Developer Console
2. Drop in a Google Gemini API key — the free tier is fine for this
3. Share your target Google Sheet with the service account email, or connect via OAuth
4. Open the "Notify team on Slack" node and set your channel name
5. Connect Gmail via OAuth in the credentials panel
6. Send a test business card photo to your LINE bot and check the Sheet

### Customization

Tweak the Gemini prompt in "Extract business card data with Gemini" to capture extra fields like LinkedIn URL or department. Adjust the Slack message blocks or the Gmail body to match your team's tone.

## Receive & validate

Picks up the LINE message and checks that an image was actually attached before doing anything else.

## Extract contact data

Downloads the image from LINE and runs it through Gemini Vision to pull out structured contact fields.

## Save to Sheets

Parses the Gemini response and appends a new contact row to your Google Sheet.

## Notify & confirm

Pings Slack with the contact summary, sends a Gmail thank-you if an email was found, then confirms back to the user on LINE.

## 📧 Send Email
If an email address was found on the business card, Gmail automatically sends a thank-you email to the new contact.

## 🔔 Slack Notification
Posts the extracted contact details to a Slack channel to keep the whole team in the loop.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
